title: "Double.longBitsToDouble" Should Take "long" as Argument in Java
description: In Java, the method Double.longBitsToDouble converts a bit pattern into its corresponding floating-point representation. It expects a 64-bit long argument to interpret the bits as a double value correctly. If the argument is of a smaller data type, the cast to long may lead to a different value than expected due to the interpretation of the most significant bit. This, in turn, can result in Double.longBitsToDouble returning an incorrect value.
recommendation: Ensure that the argument passed to the Double.longBitsToDouble method is of 'long' data type.
impact: Using a smaller data type as an argument for the Double.longBitsToDouble method can result in unexpected output due to improper bit interpretation.

checks.message: Double.longBitsToDouble should take a long argument, not a smaller data type.
